sources.title: The 50/30/20 Rule: How to Budget (Even on a Low Income)
key_points: Ever feel like your paycheck is playing
hideand seek with your bills and winning
every single time? You get paid, you're

carousels.concept: The 50/30/20 rule, rebuilt as five screenshot-worthy moves.

slides_en.headline: Your paycheck has a leak.
slides_en.body: Five fixes. One for each slide. Screenshot the one that hits.
slides_en.visual_direction: Dramatic gold-on-navy cityscape chart. High contrast, cinematic. The scroll-stopper.

slides_en.headline: Spend 50, play 30, keep 20.
slides_en.body: Needs under half your take-home. Wants under a third. The last 20% leaves the account before you see it.
slides_en.visual_direction: Notebook + calculator + finance plan sketch. Warm, planning-desk energy.

slides_en.headline: Move the 20% on payday.
slides_en.body: Set a standing transfer the morning your salary lands. If it sits in checking, it gets spent.
slides_en.visual_direction: Laptop with banking dashboard beside stacks of cash and coins. Tech-meets-money, focused.

slides_en.headline: Starbucks isn't a need.
slides_en.body: Rent, groceries, utilities, transport, insurance — that's needs. Everything else is a want wearing a disguise.
slides_en.visual_direction: Stacked bills beside a laptop, warm window light. Conceptual — the 'aha' image. Slight pattern interrupt.

slides_en.headline: Debt at 22% eats the plan.
slides_en.body: If a card charges more than your savings earn, redirect the 20% there first. The math won't work until that hole is closed.
slides_en.visual_direction: Cash stack, coins, pen, business chart. Clean desk composition. Focused, decisive.

slides_en.headline: $50 a month becomes $8,300.
slides_en.body: Ten years. Seven percent return. That's the math on starting small and not stopping.
slides_en.visual_direction: Wooden upward chart, coin, hundred-dollar bills, calculator. Growth imagery. Resolved, clear.

slides_en.headline: Save this. Run payday one.
slides_en.body: Money explained like math, not morality. Follow @personalfinancetips for one carousel a week.
slides_en.visual_direction: Solid Deep Navy. Cream headline, gold CTA button. No background photo.

slides_he.headline: במשכורת שלך יש דליפה.
slides_he.body: חמישה תיקונים. אחד בכל שקף. תצלם את זה שהכי מדבר אליך.
slides_he.visual_direction: Dramatic gold-on-navy cityscape chart. High contrast, cinematic. The scroll-stopper.

slides_he.headline: 50 לחובה, 30 לכיף, 20 לך.
slides_he.body: חצי מהנטו לחשבונות הקבועים. שליש למותרות. 20% עוברים לחיסכון בהוראת קבע, לפני שאתה רואה אותם.
slides_he.visual_direction: Notebook + calculator + finance plan sketch. Warm, planning-desk energy.

slides_he.headline: הוראת קבע ב־1 לחודש.
slides_he.body: תגדיר העברה אוטומטית לקרן כספית בבוקר שהמשכורת נכנסת. כסף שיושב בעו״ש נגמר עד סוף החודש.
slides_he.visual_direction: Laptop with banking dashboard beside stacks of cash and coins. Tech-meets-money, focused.

slides_he.headline: ארומה זה לא חשבון חובה.
slides_he.body: שכירות, סופר, חשבונות, תחבורה, ביטוחים. זו רשימת החובה. כל השאר זה רצון שמתחפש לצורך.
slides_he.visual_direction: Stacked bills beside a laptop, warm window light. Conceptual — the 'aha' image. Slight pattern interrupt.

slides_he.headline: מינוס ב־12% אוכל את הכל.
slides_he.body: אם האשראי גובה ממך יותר ממה שהחיסכון מרוויח, ה־20% הולכים לסגור אותו קודם. שום חישוב לא יעבוד עד שהחור סגור.
slides_he.visual_direction: Cash stack, coins, pen, business chart. Clean desk composition. Focused, decisive.

slides_he.headline: 200 ש״ח בחודש = 33,000.
slides_he.body: עשר שנים, 7% תשואה שנתית. קרן כספית או ETF סנופי, בלי לגעת, בלי להפסיק.
slides_he.visual_direction: Wooden upward chart, coin, hundred-dollar bills, calculator. Growth imagery. Resolved, clear.

slides_he.headline: תשמור. ותריץ ב־1 לחודש.
slides_he.body: כסף מוסבר כמו מתמטיקה, לא כמו מוסר. עקבו אחרי @personalfinancetips לקרוסלה אחת בשבוע.
slides_he.visual_direction: Solid Deep Navy. Cream headline, gold CTA button. No background photo.

carousels.concept: Rent is eating your budget — five moves when 50% doesn't fit.

slides_en.headline: Rent ate 60% of your paycheck.
slides_en.body: The 50/30/20 rule can't fix that alone. Here's what does.
slides_en.visual_direction: Cinematic navy city skyline with rising chart. Tension, scale, pressure. The scroll-stopper.

slides_en.headline: Wants budget goes to zero.
slides_en.body: Not forever. Just until needs drop below half your income. Every cancelled subscription is rent money back.
slides_en.visual_direction: Open notebook with budget sketches, coffee, calculator. Planning mode. Quiet focus.

slides_en.headline: Save $10 before you pay rent.
slides_en.body: Transfer it the second your salary hits. You'll adjust to what's left faster than you think.
slides_en.visual_direction: Laptop banking dashboard, cash stacks, handwritten notes. Action-oriented desk scene.

slides_en.headline: Stop cutting. Start earning.
slides_en.body: You can only cut down to zero. Income has no ceiling. One extra shift beats a month of skipped coffees.
slides_en.visual_direction: Stacked bills with laptop, warm light through window. The reframe moment. Different energy from prior slides.

slides_en.headline: Cash envelopes beat willpower.
slides_en.body: Withdraw your wants budget in cash on day one. When the envelope is empty, the month is over.
slides_en.visual_direction: Cash bundle, coins, green notebook, pen on business chart. Tactile, analog, disciplined.

slides_en.headline: Three months in a high-yield account.
slides_en.body: Not under a mattress. Not in checking. A separate high-yield savings account, named something boring so you don't touch it.
slides_en.visual_direction: Wooden upward chart with bills and coin. Growth, stability, resolution.

slides_en.headline: Save this. Rework the month.
slides_en.body: Money explained like math, not morality. Follow @personalfinancetips for one carousel a week.
slides_en.visual_direction: Solid Deep Navy. Cream headline, gold CTA button. No background photo.

slides_he.headline: שכירות אוכלת 60% מהנטו?
slides_he.body: 50/30/20 לא יסדר לך את זה לבד. חמישה מהלכים שכן.
body_emphasis: לא יסדר לך את זה לבד
slides_he.visual_direction: Cinematic navy city skyline with rising chart. Tension, scale, pressure. The scroll-stopper.

slides_he.headline: תקציב המותרות יורד לאפס.
slides_he.body: לא לתמיד. רק עד שהחובה יורדת מתחת לחצי מהנטו. כל מנוי שביטלת חוזר כשקל לשכירות.
slides_he.visual_direction: Open notebook with budget sketches, coffee, calculator. Planning mode. Quiet focus.

slides_he.headline: 50 ש״ח לחיסכון לפני השכירות.
slides_he.body: הוראת קבע לקרן כספית, בשנייה שהמשכורת נכנסת. תתרגל לחיות ממה שנשאר מהר ממה שחשבת.
slides_he.visual_direction: Laptop banking dashboard, cash stacks, handwritten notes. Action-oriented desk scene.

slides_he.headline: תפסיק לקצץ. תתחיל להרוויח.
slides_he.body: אפשר לקצץ עד אפס. להכנסה אין תקרה. משמרת אחת נוספת שווה יותר מחודש של ויתור על קפה.
slides_he.visual_direction: Stacked bills with laptop, warm light through window. The reframe moment. Different energy from prior slides.

slides_he.headline: מזומן במעטפה מנצח כוח רצון.
slides_he.body: תמשוך את תקציב המותרות במזומן ב־1 לחודש. כשהמעטפה ריקה, החודש נגמר. בלי להתווכח עם עצמך.
slides_he.visual_direction: Cash bundle, coins, green notebook, pen on business chart. Tactile, analog, disciplined.

slides_he.headline: שלושה חודשים בקרן כספית.
slides_he.body: לא מתחת למזרן. לא בעו״ש. חשבון נפרד בקרן כספית, עם שם משעמם בכוונה שלא תתפתה לגעת בו.
slides_he.visual_direction: Wooden upward chart with bills and coin. Growth, stability, resolution.

slides_he.headline: תשמור. ותבנה את החודש מחדש.
slides_he.body: כסף מוסבר כמו מתמטיקה, לא כמו מוסר. עקבו אחרי @personalfinancetips לקרוסלה אחת בשבוע.
slides_he.visual_direction: Solid Deep Navy. Cream headline, gold CTA button. No background photo.

recommendations_for_dashboard.rendering_notes: Fonts: Fraunces+Inter (EN), Frank Ruhl Libre+Assistant (HE). Canvas 1080x1350 portrait.
recommendations_for_dashboard.next_carousels_run_suggestion: Run a debt-payoff counter-frame carousel: why the avalanche method beats the snowball on paper but loses to it in real life — five behavioral rules for actually killing a credit card.
